edges.description: Pathogenic ST3GAL5 variants abolish or markedly reduce GM3 synthase enzyme activity.
edges.source: ST3GAL5 GM3 synthase loss of function

edges.description: Loss of GM3 synthase activity depletes GM3 and biosynthetic derivatives.
edges.source: ST3GAL5 GM3 synthase loss of function

edges.description: Loss of GM3 synthase causes absent or markedly reduced GM3 ganglioside in affected individuals.
Because GM3 is upstream of most complex gangliosides, downstream ganglioside species are depleted.
Ganglioside depletion shifts glycosphingolipid composition and glycosylation programs.
Complex ganglioside loss alters cell-surface signaling in neural lineage cells.
Loss of GM3-derived gangliosides produces a measurable abnormal glycosphingolipid profile in patient-derived cells.
Altered glycosphingolipid profiles perturb membrane and receptor signaling states.
Glycolipid and glycoprotein glycosylation remodeling is associated with the neurocutaneous pigmentation phenotype.
Dysmorphic facial features are reported in ST3GAL5-related neurocutaneous disease, but the cached evidence does not resolve the intermediate morphogenetic mechanism.
Neural membrane and signaling disruption contributes to severe infantile epilepsy.
Neural-lineage signaling and survival defects contribute to severe developmental impairment.
Neural tissue vulnerability contributes to cortical visual impairment and optic pathway disease.
Ganglioside biology in neural tissues is linked to the sensorineural phenotype.
Severe neural dysfunction in infancy is associated with prominent irritability.
Early neurologic disease is associated with poor feeding in infancy.
Infantile neurologic disease and poor feeding are associated with failure to thrive.
Feeding difficulty, failure to thrive, and severe neurodevelopmental disease are associated with postnatal growth impairment.
Neural-lineage signaling and survival defects are associated with developmental regression in the progressive disease course.
Severe neural dysfunction is associated with profound intellectual disability.
Neural and optic pathway vulnerability is associated with optic atrophy or pale papillae.
Neural signaling vulnerability is associated with hyperkinetic movement disorder including choreoathetosis.
Neural motor system involvement is associated with hypotonic tetraparesis.
Scoliosis is reported in ST3GAL5-related salt-and-pepper syndrome, but the cached evidence does not resolve the intermediate skeletal or neuromotor mechanism.
Severe neurodevelopmental disease is associated with progressive microcephaly.
Reduced GM3 synthase activity reports the proximal ST3GAL5 enzymatic defect.
edges.source: ST3GAL5 GM3 synthase loss of function

edges.description: Absent GM3 ganglioside reports failure of the ST3GAL5-dependent biosynthetic step.
Depletion of downstream gangliosides reports the broader biosynthetic consequence of absent GM3 synthase activity.
Altered GSL profiles report compensatory glycosphingolipid remodeling downstream of GM3 depletion.
